page: Microsoft Azure E32as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU.

The server includes 524 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPUs. The AMD EPYC processor supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2707.99 and a strong PassMark memory score of 2782.36. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average tier. While the system handles small-model LLM text generation efficiently at 310.9 tokens/sec, it exhibits weak performance on large-model prompt processing, registering 6.62 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32as_v4 offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the premium cost of GPU-accelerated hardware. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and high memory benchmark scores, while multi-core operations, database tasks, and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The server lacks GPU accelerators and AVX-512 support, which limits its efficiency for heavy deep learning workloads, as evidenced by weak large-model LLM prompt processing. However, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications. The E32as_v4 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database management, in-memory caching, and general enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, delivering strong single-core performance for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high memory density for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Includes 524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise applications
